sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("12-pixel objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 12:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) color={n['color']}")

print("\nWhite objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So clicking on (23,37) moved the white border to (23,37). The white border was reactivated. Now I have:
- (11,34) G
- (23,37) W
- (42,40) G
- (51,40) G
So the mechanic is: clicking on a gray-bordered object moves the white border to it. This is like "selecting" that object.
Now I need to figure out how to remove objects. Earlier, clicking on the orange object (28,37) removed (35,45) when white was at (35,45). But when white was at (11,34), clicking on orange didn't remove (11,34).
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the removal only happens when clicking on a SPECIFIC trail segment or object, not the orange object itself.
Let me look at the trail structure more carefully. The trail connects:
- (11,34) - top
- (23,37) - upper middle
- Orange object (28,37) - junction
- Purple object (35,38) - lower middle
- (42,40) - lower
- (51,40) - bottom
When I click on a trail segment, the white border moves along the trail. When I click on a bordered object, the white border moves to that object.
The question is: how do I remove objects?
Looking at the history:
1. Click (42,40): white moved to (42,40), (48,54) removed - (48,54) was the previous white-bordered object
2. Click (23,41): white moved to (23,41), (9,49) removed - (9,49) was the previous white-bordered object
3. Click (28,37): white moved to (35,45)... no wait, (35,45) was removed
Actually, let me reconsider. When I clicked on (42,40), the white border was at (48,54). After clicking, white moved to (42,40) and (48,54) was removed. So clicking on a bordered object moves the white border there and removes the PREVIOUS white-bordered object.
When I clicked on (23,41), the white border was at (9,49). After clicking, white moved to (23,41) and (9,49) was removed. Same pattern.
When I clicked on (28,37) (orange), the white border was at (35,45). After clicking, (35,45) was removed. But where did the white border go? It seems like it was removed along with (35,45).
When I clicked on (28,37) (orange) again, the white border was at (11,34). After clicking, (11,34) became gray (not removed). The white border was removed.
So the pattern seems to be:
- Clicking on a bordered object moves the white border there and removes the PREVIOUS white-bordered object
- Clicking on the orange object removes the current white-bordered object (but only the first time?)
Wait, but when I clicked on orange the second time, (11,34) was NOT removed. So maybe the orange object can only remove one object?
Or maybe the orange object's behavior depends on the position of the purple object?
Let me try clicking on the orange object now with white at (23,37) to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on orange object (28,37) with white at (23,37)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 28, 'col': 37}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
